message:
chore(beads): Mark bd-2ikc (@ fuzzy file reference) as in_progress

Update issue tracker to reflect active work on the Editor @ fuzzy file
reference feature (bd-2ikc). The issue transitions from "open" to
"in_progress" as implementation begins on the fuzzy-search project
files UX triggered by typing @ in the editor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2hz","title":"Extension UI protocol integration (interactive + RPC)","description":"Background:
- Legacy spec defines extension_ui_request/extension_ui_response (confirm/select/input/editor/notify/etc.) and blocking semantics.
- EXTENSIONS.md mandates Node/Bun-free runtime with capability-gated connectors and deterministic logs.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement runtime plumbing so extension UI requests surface in interactive TUI (prompt/confirm/select/editor) with timeout + cancellation.
2) Implement RPC mode passthrough: emit extension_ui_request on stdout; accept extension_ui_response on stdin.
3) Enforce request/response ID mapping, default values, and timeouts; normalize errors.
4) Add conformance fixtures for each UI method + timeout/cancel edge cases; wire into tests.
5) Emit structured logs (pi.ext.log.v1) for UI requests/responses with correlation IDs.

Acceptance:
- A sample extension can invoke confirm/select/input/editor and receive responses in both interactive + RPC modes.
- Timeouts and cancellation propagate correctly without deadlocks.
- Fixtures pass and logs are deterministic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.808698612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:36.834579243Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hz","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2i2u","title":"Unit tests: provider factory + OpenAI base URL normalization","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for src/providers/mod.rs factory selection and OpenAI base URL normalization.

# Scope / Deliverables
- create_provider returns Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini providers with expected name/api/model_id.
- Azure OpenAI path returns the explicit error (resource+deployment).
- Unknown provider returns Error::provider with clear message.
- normalize_openai_base handles:
  - base URL ending with /v1
  - already /chat/completions or /responses
  - trailing slashes and missing path

# No-Mock Rule
- Use real ModelEntry structs; no fake providers or network calls.

# Logging
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log input base URLs and normalized outputs.

# Acceptance
- 10+ tests covering factory selection and base URL normalization edge cases.
- Deterministic, offline, no network.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T20:28:53.257378023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:15:13.022586880Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2i2u","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i2u","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":178,"issue_id":"bd-2i2u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented provider factory + OpenAI base URL normalization tests in tests/provider_factory.rs (10 test cases total). Exposed normalize_openai_base for test visibility in src/providers/mod.rs. Ran cargo fmt/check/clippy; clippy warns only from asupersync missing_docs.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:15:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2i5","title":"Wire extension runtime into agent loop (tools/commands/events)","description":"Background:
- Extensions must integrate with the agent loop so their tools and commands are callable.

Steps:
- Register extension tools with the ToolRegistry and route tool_call/tool_result.
- Register slash commands and hook them into interactive mode.
- Implement event hook emission at appropriate agent lifecycle points.
- Ensure wiring supports deterministic test hooks (ordering + timing).

Logging requirements:
- Emit structured logs for tool registration, command invocation, and event hooks (correlation IDs).

Acceptance:
- An extension-provided tool and slash command can be invoked end-to-end.
- Logs follow the extension logging spec for conformance/E2E tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:12.588180426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:07:05.988120840Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-320","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i5","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2ikc","title":"Editor: @ fuzzy file reference insertion","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `@` file reference UX: typing `@` triggers fuzzy-search project files.

# Behavioral Spec
- As the user types after `@`, suggestions narrow using fuzzy matching.
- Selecting a file inserts a usable reference into the editor.

# Open Design Choice (resolve in implementation)
Legacy mentions “File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files”.
We must choose one of:
1) Insert `@relative/path` (explicit marker), OR
2) Insert `relative/path` but still treat it as an attachment later.

Pick one and document it; ensure it works with existing file-context inclusion code.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can attach files without leaving the keyboard.
- [ ] Inserted reference is correct relative to cwd and works across platforms.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:34.583235605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:40.656752819Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-28rk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-28t8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2ikc","title":"Editor: @ fuzzy file reference insertion","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `@` file reference UX: typing `@` triggers fuzzy-search project files.

# Behavioral Spec
- As the user types after `@`, suggestions narrow using fuzzy matching.
- Selecting a file inserts a usable reference into the editor.

# Open Design Choice (resolve in implementation)
Legacy mentions “File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files”.
We must choose one of:
1) Insert `@relative/path` (explicit marker), OR
2) Insert `relative/path` but still treat it as an attachment later.

Pick one and document it; ensure it works with existing file-context inclusion code.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can attach files without leaving the keyboard.
- [ ] Inserted reference is correct relative to cwd and works across platforms.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:39:34.583235605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:41:54.742945768Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-28rk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ikc","depends_on_id":"bd-28t8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jdz","title":"Interactive: implement /new (start new session without restart)","description":"# Goal
Implement `/new` inside interactive mode to start a fresh session without restarting the process.

# Required Behavior
- Clear the current conversation view.
- Create a new Session:
  - new session id
  - new JSONL file path (if persistence enabled)
  - header uses current cwd, model/thinking settings
- Reset agent message history to an empty session context.

# UX
- Preserve loaded resources (skills/prompts/themes) from current run.
- Provide a status message indicating new session started.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/new` results in a clean slate session.
- [ ] Persistence works (new file created, not overwriting old).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:34.248604864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:05:20.484206305Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:05:20.484141665Z","close_reason":"Implemented /new interactive command; resets conversation + new session persistence","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jdz","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jh","title":"Implement error handling path tests","description":"# Implement error handling path tests

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures
- API rate limits
- Authentication failures
- Malformed responses
- File system errors
- Timeout scenarios

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- All API error cases must use VCR playback (no live network).
- File system errors must use temp dirs and deterministic paths.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log cassette path, error category, and sanitized error details.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual error variants + any response body fragments.

## Test Categories

### Network Errors
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_connection_refused() {
    let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("network_connection_refused");
    let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

    let result = provider.stream(&context, &options).await;
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(Error::Network { .. })));
}
```

### API Errors
- 429 rate limit (retry-after)
- 401 auth failure
- 403 forbidden
- 400 bad request
- 500 server error
- 529 overloaded

### Malformed Response Errors
- Invalid JSON body
- Missing required fields
- Unexpected SSE event type
- Truncated stream

### File System Errors
- Read permission denied
- Write permission denied
- Disk full (simulate with tempfs limits if feasible)
- Path too long

### Tool Execution Errors
- bash command not found
- bash timeout
- edit file not found

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure for API error testing)

## Files
- tests/error_handling.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 25+ error handling tests
- [ ] All network errors covered (via VCR/local simulation)
- [ ] All API error codes covered
- [ ] File system errors covered
- [ ] Tool errors covered
- [ ] Logs include error category + cassette id
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:33.110276433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:34.660912407Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ke","title":"Impl: QuickJS promise bridge + job draining semantics","description":"# Goal
Wire QuickJS async runtime to the PiJS event loop with a **correct Promise/hostcall bridge** and explicit job draining.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `host.call` returns a JS Promise backed by a Rust completion handle.
- Job draining loop: run pending jobs, then poll timers/hostcalls per spec.
- Cancellation propagation: cancel token to hostcall, reject Promise with mapped error.
- Explicit error mapping + stack traces for debugging.
- Structured logs per hostcall with correlation IDs.

# Correctness Notes
- No re-entrancy hazards: only drain jobs at safe points.
- Promise resolution order stable with scheduler sequence ids.

# Tests
- Unit tests for Promise resolution ordering.
- Conformance fixtures for cancellation/timeouts.
- E2E lifecycle harness asserts log ordering.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:13.010362542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:07:11.626074378Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:01:18.379160588Z","close_reason":"Implemented QuickJS promise bridge with job draining semantics. Created PiJsRuntime combining QuickJS + Scheduler + Promise bridge. Added 5 integration tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ke","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"bd-2ke","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented PiJsRuntime Promise/hostcall bridge + job draining semantics; updated json<->js conversion and added Promise bridge tests. Landed in commits: 52e33cd, 7a357f1, 0b0958a.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:07:11Z"}]}
